</think>

    - paragraph:
      - text: The ACM A. M. Turing Award is an annual prize given by the
      - link "Association for Computing Machinery" [ref=e65] [nth=1]:
        - /url: /wiki/Association_for_Computing_Machinery
      - text: (ACM) for contributions of lasting and major technical importance to
      - link "computer science" [ref=e66] [nth=1]:
        - /url: /wiki/Computer_science
      - text: . It is generally recognized as the highest distinction in the field of computer science and is often referred to as the "
      - link "Nobel Prize of Computing" [ref=e67]:
        - /url: /wiki/List_of_awards_considered_the_highest_in_a_field
      - text: "\". As of 2025, 79 people have been awarded the prize, with the most recent recipients being"
      - link "Andrew Barto" [ref=e68]:
        - /url: /wiki/Andrew_Barto
      - text: and
      - link "Richard S. Sutton" [ref=e69]:
        - /url: /wiki/Richard_S._Sutton
      - text: ", who won in 2024."
      - superscript:
        - link "[2]" [ref=e70]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-ACM-2"
      - superscript:
        - link "[3]" [ref=e71]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-BartoSutton-3"
      - superscript:
        - link "[4]" [ref=e72]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-4"
      - superscript:
        - link "[5]" [ref=e73]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-5"
      - superscript:
        - link "[6]" [ref=e74]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-6"
    - paragraph:
      - text: The award is named after
      - link "Alan Turing" [ref=e75] [nth=1]:
        - /url: /wiki/Alan_Turing
      - text: ", also referred as \"Father of Computer Science\", who was a British mathematician and"
      - link "reader" [ref=e76]:
        - /url: /wiki/Reader_(academic_rank)
      - text: in mathematics at the
      - link "University of Manchester" [ref=e77]:
        - /url: /wiki/University_of_Manchester
      - text: . Turing is often credited as being the founder of
      - link "theoretical computer science" [ref=e78]:
        - /url: /wiki/Theoretical_computer_science
      - text: and
      - link "artificial intelligence" [ref=e79]:
        - /url: /wiki/Artificial_intelligence
      - text: ","
      - superscript:
        - link "[7]" [ref=e80]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-7"
      - text: and a key contributor to the Allied
      - link "cryptanalysis" [ref=e81]:
        - /url: /wiki/Cryptanalysis
      - text: of the
      - link "Enigma cipher" [ref=e82]:
        - /url: /wiki/Enigma_cipher
      - text: during
      - link "World War II" [ref=e83]:
        - /url: /wiki/World_War_II
      - text: .
      - superscript:
        - link "[8]" [ref=e84]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-bbc-copeland-8"
      - text: From 2007 to 2013, the award was accompanied by a prize of US$250,000, with financial support provided by
      - link "Intel" [ref=e85]:
        - /url: /wiki/Intel
      - text: and
      - link "Google" [ref=e86]:
        - /url: /wiki/Google
      - text: .
      - superscript:
        - link "[2]" [ref=e87] [nth=1]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-ACM-2"
      - superscript:
        - link "[9]" [ref=e88]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-9"
      - text: Since 2014, the award has been accompanied by a prize of US$1 million, with financial support provided by Google.
      - superscript:
        - link "[1]" [ref=e89] [nth=1]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-million-1"
      - superscript:
        - link "[10]" [ref=e90]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-10"
    - paragraph:
      - text: The first recipient, in 1966, was
      - link "Alan Perlis" [ref=e91]:
        - /url: /wiki/Alan_Perlis
      - text: . The youngest recipient was
      - link "Donald Knuth" [ref=e92]:
        - /url: /wiki/Donald_Knuth
      - text: ", who won in 1974 at the age of 36,"
      - superscript:
        - link "[11]" [ref=e93]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-11"
      - text: while the oldest recipient was
      - link "Alfred Aho" [ref=e94]:
        - /url: /wiki/Alfred_Aho
      - text: ", who won in 2020 at the age of 79."
      - superscript:
        - link "[12]" [ref=e95]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-12"
      - text: "Only three women have been awarded the prize:"
      - link "Frances Allen" [ref=e96]:
        - /url: /wiki/Frances_Allen
      - text: (in 2006),
      - superscript:
        - link "[13]" [ref=e97]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-Allen-13"
      - link "Barbara Liskov" [ref=e98]:
        - /url: /wiki/Barbara_Liskov
      - text: (in 2008),
      - superscript:
        - link "[14]" [ref=e99]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-liskov-14"
      - text: and
      - link "Shafi Goldwasser" [ref=e100]:
        - /url: /wiki/Shafi_Goldwasser
      - text: (in 2012).
      - superscript:
        - link "[15]" [ref=e101]:
          - /url: "#cite_note-goldwasser-15"
